title: String Functions
content:
### String Functions

String functions manipulate text data. They are used for cleaning, formatting, and extracting information from strings.

---

### Case Conversion

| Function | Description | Example |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| UPPER(str) | Converts to uppercase | UPPER('sql') => 'SQL' |
| LOWER(str) | Converts to lowercase | LOWER('SQL') => 'sql' |
| INITCAP(str) | Capitalizes first letter of each word | INITCAP('hello world') => 'Hello World' |

### String Inspection

| Function | Description | Example |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| LENGTH(str) | Returns character count | LENGTH('hello') => 5 |
| CHAR_LENGTH(str) | Returns character count (multi-byte safe) | CHAR_LENGTH('cafe') => 4 |
| POSITION(substr IN str) | Finds position of substring | POSITION('world' IN 'hello world') => 7 |

### String Manipulation

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| CONCAT(a, b, c) | Joins strings together |
| SUBSTRING(str, start, length) | Extracts a portion of a string |
| REPLACE(str, from, to) | Replaces occurrences of a substring |
| TRIM(str) | Removes leading and trailing spaces |
| LTRIM(str) | Removes leading spaces |
| RTRIM(str) | Removes trailing spaces |
| LPAD(str, len, pad) | Left-pads string to specified length |
| RPAD(str, len, pad) | Right-pads string to specified length |

```sql
SELECT CONCAT(first_name, ' ', last_name) AS full_name,
       SUBSTRING(email, 1, POSITION('@' IN email) - 1) AS username,
       REPLACE(phone, '-', '') AS clean_phone
FROM employees;
```

> **Key Point:** String positions in SQL typically start at 1, not 0. Some databases use || as the concatenation operator instead of CONCAT().

quiz.question: Which function combines multiple strings together?
